body_txt: A harsh character breaks down into tears so the audience can easily sympathize with them in their moment of emotional vulnerability. The tougher a character is, the more emotional it is when a Sparkling Stream of Tears starts pouring out of their eyes. The Jerkass / tyrant/ stoic / tsundere instantaneously becomes The Woobie , and their endearingly vulnerable sobs and pleas for sympathy make the coldest of hearts want to comfort them. Sometimes this moment acts as a road stop in a Break the Haughty arc, a turning point in which the character starts to slowly become a better person . However, other times, having felt embarrassed for daring to be vulnerable, the character might immediately thereafter worsen their attitude. In the case of male characters, this trope is different from Manly Tears which requires a man crying for dignified reasons, and as such is usually applied to already decent male characters. In contrast, Humanizing Tears is about creating sympathy through vulnerability rather than kindness, so a Jerkass crying over their own misery, even in the case where they're solely to blame for it, can still be a humanizing moment, unless they're fishing for sympathy through Crocodile Tears in which case it's this trope. If this applies to an already sympathetic Nice Guy , then this is Tender Tears . This can also be used to humanize an entire kind or a faction. For example, Mooks , especially the ones that wear masks are usually seen as obstacles in the way of an Action Hero who won't mind slaughtering hundred of them and the audience won't think much of it either. And then, one scene shows a lone mook crying over his fallen friends, perhaps with his mask still on to hammer down that he's not more significant than the other but that these people were all humans with loved ones and dreams of their own. See also Tears of Remorse , where somebody cries for feeling guilty; and Tears from a Stone , where a seemingly inanimate or emotionless being cries (it may also apply as this trope if used to humanize said being). This is often a sign of Cracks in the Icy Façade , as well as a Humanizing Moment . A Super-Trope to Cry into Chest . Contrast with Inelegant Blubbering and Crocodile Tears .
